title: pilosella brachycoma subsp. colluta
intro: Pilosella brachycoma subsp. colluta, also known as Rough Hawk's-beard or Colluted Pilosella, is a small, annual or biennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Pilosella brachycoma subsp. colluta include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. It is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and can also be consumed as a tea or added to salves and creams for topical application.
